strategy_doc_section_loaded: content-strategy-agentic-engineering-101.md § 'M2 in detail — plan mode, done right' (especially the Second exercise paragraph and the Why-both-fire paragraph) + § 'Recurring themes' (capture-your-world #5 + non-agentic-default #6) + § 'The Compound step is visible in every module' (M2 save-if-earned shape)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Mood contract named — honour, not flatten
Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
Strategy-fidelity — no front-running of downstream modules
Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
Key Concepts shape — theme breadcrumbs, not standalone section
What You'll Learn shape — capability register, strategic-theme verbs lead
Big Idea match (strategy doc Big Idea vs file)

notes: Exercise file (not module file) — Key Concepts (§6) and What You'll Learn (§7) live at module-file scope and are N/A here. File body's 'The point:' line ('the file is the artifact. You captured how you factored this task on this codebase') honours the strategy doc's Big Idea framing for the second M2 exercise. Mood maintainer-block state ('the rule sits in MY file at MY location; I see how this could automate; I'm not building it today — and that's the move') matches strategy doc verbatim. Strategy-fidelity: file's Non-front-running checks (Phase 2 names .md not skill — M3 owns first skill; Phase 3 names shapes not invocations — M5/M6 own verifier + eval-encoding) maps 1:1 to strategy doc constraints. Themes earned block names capture-your-world (#5), compounding (Block 1 observation→rule), mirror, self-aware + grain of salt, non-agentic-default — five of the six AE101 themes surfaced explicitly in maintainer rationale, with body-side embodiment (Phase 1 'name them', Phase 3 'shapes not code'). Lead-with-discipline: body verbs are capture / name / decide / save / rewrite — positive disciplines, no failure-mode framing.
